title: Implantation of a Pelvic Nerve Array in Rats
author: James B Fallon
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfwxjpfn

steps.title: Prior to surgery, pelvic nerve arrays...
steps.instruction: Prior to surgery, pelvic nerve arrays are sterilised through a sonication process and autoclaving.

steps.title: Sonication cycles
steps.instruction: Sonication cycles: 15 mins in 5% Pyroneg, 5 mins distilled water, 5 mins in distilled water, 10 mins in 96% ethanol, 5 mins in distilled water, 5 mins in distilled water

steps.title: Autoclave
steps.instruction: Autoclave

steps.title: For surgery, anesthetise (2
steps.instruction: For surgery, anesthetise (2.5% isoflurane in oxygen, or as required for maintenance) the animal (adult male rat Sprague-Dawley, 8-10 weeks)

steps.title: Apply eye lubricant and place animal on a heat pad
steps.instruction: Apply eye lubricant and place animal on a heat pad

steps.title: Shave and clean (betadine) the ventral...
steps.instruction: Shave and clean (betadine) the ventral abdomen and the dorsal-lumbar back

steps.title: Make an incision to the dorsal-lumbar...
steps.instruction: Make an incision to the dorsal-lumbar aspect of the back (this will be the exit site for the percutaneous electrode connector).

steps.title: Make a ventral midline incision in...
steps.instruction: Make a ventral midline incision in the skin overlying the region of the bladder

steps.title: Using hemostats, subcutaneously tunnel the array...
steps.instruction: Using hemostats, subcutaneously tunnel the array from the dorsal incision site through to the ventral incision site.

steps.title: Make an incision in the ventral...
steps.instruction: Make an incision in the ventral midline of the abdominal cavity to expose the bladder.

steps.title: Make a second, small (1 cm)...
steps.instruction: Make a second, small (1 cm) incision (lateral to the midline incision) to the abdominal muscle on the same side as lead wire, and feed the array through this secondary incision.

steps.title: Locate the left or the right...
steps.instruction: Locate the left or the right prostate and gently retract the lobe to reveal the pelvic ganglia and connecting nerves (including the pelvic nerve)

steps.title: Clearly identify the pelvic nerve
steps.instruction: Clearly identify the pelvic nerve

steps.title: The array is placed over the...
steps.instruction: The array is placed over the top of the pelvic nerve and secured in place to the connective tissue of the prostate using sutures (7’0 silk).

steps.title: Exteriorise the lead wire through the...
steps.instruction: Exteriorise the lead wire through the abdominal cavity (via the second small incision).

steps.title: Close the abdominal cavity and skin incision sites with sutures
steps.instruction: Close the abdominal cavity and skin incision sites with sutures.

steps.title: Suture (2’0 silk) the percutaneous pedestal...
steps.instruction: Suture (2’0 silk) the percutaneous pedestal silicone-Dacron embedded mesh to the underlying muscle and connective tissue of the dorsal midline near the neck.

steps.title: Close the skin incision site with sutures
steps.instruction: Close the skin incision site with sutures.

steps.title: Administer analgesic and sterile Hartmann’s solution
steps.instruction: Administer analgesic and sterile Hartmann’s solution.

steps.title: During post-surgery management, the skin around...
steps.instruction: During post-surgery management, the skin around the percutaneous plug is cleaned with betadine every 2 weeks to ensure an infection does not develop. Rats can be awake, but gently restrained, during this maintenance procedure.

steps.title: At this stage of the surgery,...
steps.instruction: At this stage of the surgery, a bladder catheter can be implanted according to the protocol described in 'Cystometry for Awake Rats' Cystometry in awake rats by Janet R Keast
